title: Chess pie
subtitle: American Southern custard pie with butter, sugar, and a hint of vinegar
excerpt: A Southern American custard pie with an intense, translucent filling made from butter, sugar, eggs, a little cornmeal, and a splash of vinegar that gives a barely perceptible tang. The filling sets to a soft, dense custard. Serves eight.
sourceNotes: Chess pie is documented in American Southern domestic cookery from the nineteenth century, with roots in British transparent puddings brought to the Southern states. The USDA Cooperative Extension Service publications on Southern baking document chess pie as a traditional preparation made from butter, sugar, eggs, and cornmeal or flour. The recipe appears in numerous post-Civil War Southern domestic cookery books in the public domain, including Annabella Hill, Mrs. Hill's New Cook Book (1867), documenting a sugar-and-egg custard pie consistent with this recipe.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Can be made a day ahead and kept at room temperature. Refrigerate in hot weather.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; the filling should be set at the edges but still tremble slightly in the centre; the top may crack slightly as it bakes; 35–40 minutes

content.text: Chess pie filling is liquid before it bakes and sets to a soft, fudgy custard as it cools; do not expect it to look done in the oven. The small amount of polenta (used here in place of the traditional cornmeal) and vinegar are the defining characteristics: the polenta gives a faint graininess to the filling and the vinegar prevents it from being cloying. The pie should be completely cool before slicing.

items.prepNote: about 2 tsp; or white wine vinegar

content.text: Make the pastry and blind bake

Rub the cold {{unsalted-butter}} (pastry portion) into the {{plain-flour}} and {{icing-sugar}} until crumbly. Add the egg yolk and 1 to 2 tablespoons of cold water and press together until the dough coheres. Flatten, wrap, and refrigerate for 30 minutes. Preheat the oven to 165°C fan. Roll the pastry to 3 mm and line the tin. Blind bake with paper and beans for 15 minutes, then remove the beans and bake for a further 5 minutes until the base is dry.

Whisk the {{caster-sugar}} and melted {{unsalted-butter}} (filling portion) together. Add the {{eggs}} (filling portion) one at a time, whisking after each. Stir in the {{double-cream}}, {{polenta}}, {{vanilla-extract}}, and {{cider-vinegar}}. Pour into the warm pastry case and bake for 35 to 40 minutes until the edges are set and the centre trembles slightly. Cool completely in the tin before slicing; at least 30 minutes.
